new_feedback.element: Inspect drawer cost chip — 'cost $0.008' painted in #57C08A success green (line 572)
new_feedback.issue: This is the same green-as-numeric inversion the right-rail 'Est. spend' was corrected for in iter-02, now recurring inside the Inspect drawer. The three stat chips at the bottom of the drawer (tokens 612, cost $0.008, latency 0.4s) are a homogeneous numeric trio, but the cost value alone is rendered success green while tokens and latency use body color #E8EDF3. Green is reserved in this palette for status verdicts (✓ sealed · valid, ✓ within policy, valid·sealed in PARSED OUTPUT). Coloring a dollar figure green semantically reads as 'this cost is the good outcome' rather than a neutral measurement, and it breaks the visual symmetry of the three sibling chips so the eye snags on cost for the wrong reason.
new_feedback.change: Render the cost value in body color #E8EDF3, matching the tokens and latency values in the same chip row. Keep #57C08A exclusively for the verdict line ('valid · sealed · 3 obligations …' in PARSED OUTPUT). The three metric chips should read as one uniform numeric band.
